On 26 April 1986 the worst nuclear accident in history occurred. This is the detailed build up to how that disaster happened. Narrated by Robert Powell.
The first decade of the 20th century would see what was known as the 'Golden Age' of railways when locomotive size would double and the railways would reach their zenith. We follow its development.
The many variants of the A-1 Skyraider single piston-engined fighter bomber helped dominate the air to ground attack role in Vietnam and earned quite the reputation. S2 E4
Hitler had a dream to amass the world's greatest art collection; the team investigates how Hitler oversaw the theft of thousands of art treasures from across Europe. S4 E3: Hitler's Obsession
James and Mike range deep into Normandy, where key later engagements took place, as they visit neglected battlegrounds and absorb the immense scale of the campaign. S1 E3: Breakout

programmes.show_name: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programmes.show_description: Examining the rise of the Nazis and their impact. Fear and hatred of Bolshevism drove many Germans to fight to the bitter end despite continued suffering. Ep6: Fighting To The End
To celebrate the centenary of the Royal Air Force, Ewan and Colin McGregor meet people from throughout its history and take to the skies in some of the world's most iconic planes.
Battleships were seen as the key weapon at sea at the beginning of World War II, but their vulnerability was exposed by submarines and aircraft over the next six years. Ep7: The Battleship
There are some news events that change the world; we go back to the 20th of July, 1969 and take a look at man's quest to land on the Moon.
We examine the beginning of the War and the involvement of multiple countries in both sides of the battle leading to the two-front war and the deployment of new weapons. E2
A look at the rise and fall of the London docks, once the commercial hub of Britain during the days of the old Empire, and enabled riches to be brought to the capital with a constant stream of ships.
The countdown of America's most infamous mysteries continues and looks at General Custer's last stand, explores who whacked Bugsy Sigel, and investigates a deadly attack on Wall Street in 1920. E2
The strike continues as Bill works out that a coal seam runs under his house, and Tom pursues two kids who have stolen from him but takes pity on them when he discovers why. S1 E5: Coal Comfort
Arthur plans to boost the membership of the Winchester Club, but ends up in trouble over a stolen karaoke machine. S8 E9: The Greatest Show in Willesden
Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
At the start of a new school year, Mr. Brown has two new students... as well as ten old ones who did not pass their exam. S2 E1: All Present If Not Correct
DCI Gene Hunt immediately smells a rat when his former Manchester colleagues DCI Litton and DI Bevan turn up on his patch. S3 E5
Alf's eyesight is worsening, which is fine until he accidentally enters the Ladies' toilet and is arrested as a sex pest. S1 E2

Flight 007 strayed into Soviet airspace and shot down, mistaken for a spy plane. Accident or deliberate act. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Following the Great War the railways of Great Britain had to regroup, forming four large companies from 1923 known as the 'Big 4'. We follow the progress as each of them developed their own style.
The definitive combat helicopter saw first blood in South East Asia and has been transport, gunship, smoke ship, command centre, MediVac and dozens of other roles. S2 E5
Looted gold was the lifeblood of the Nazis as Hitler used stolen gold to fund the Nazi war machine - without it, World War II might never have happened. S4 E4: Nazi Gold
The closing years of the Second World War; as the D-Day invasion of Normandy brought victory one step closer, people on the Home Front continued to endure rationing and German rocket attacks.
This episode reveals how Hitler manipulated and played games with the Czechs and with the western powers of Britain and France to achieve his ends. Ep1
A definitive film history of British forces in action, from D-Day to the Normandy breakout, featuring newly-discovered film from the Imperial War Museum and the Movietone archive.
Rockets had a huge impact on World War II - hand-held rocket launchers such as the American bazooka and German panzerfaust enabled infantry to tackle tanks head-on. Ep8: Rockets
We look at one of the deadliest storms in United States history which struck the nation's southern coast in August 2005. Hurricane Katrina rampaged through the Gulf Coast communities.
A look at the recruitment and deployment of troops on both sides of the clash, the problems faced by the soldiers, the slow pace of the fighting and the establishment of trench warfare. E3
The mighty Flying Fortress takes to the air as we examine the background and development of one of the most iconic bombers from WWII. Serving with both the USAAF and the RAF. S2 E1

Our countdown of America's biggest mysteries continues with the story of the death of the first man of magic Harry Houdini, one of the old West's most prolific gunslingers, and the great moon hoax. E3
The strike ends with no concessions won by the miners, Jessie's headmaster proposes to her, and Matt tells Jack that Dolly is pregnant and agrees to marry her. S1 E6: Empire Day on the Slag Heap
Arthur enters the food business, but when the chef he employs goes on the run from a group of gangsters, Arthur is left to cater for a policeman's retirement party. S8 E10: Too Many Crooks
Rigsby is soon calling the Samaritans to stop a suicide attempt when a new lodger arrives at the house, but which of the residents will come off worse? S3 E4: The Good Samaritans
After an attempt to stop a prison riot goes badly wrong and one of their own is left behind, the team try everything they can to save their colleague. S3 E6
Alf feels that he and Else are entitled to home help. However, it seems that the perfect person for the job is a very flamboyant gay black man. S1 E3
